scenarios.title: Document To Markdown Conversion
scenarios.when_to_read: Tasks involving MarkItDown conversion of files, streams, URIs, Office/PDF/HTML/text archives, plugins, or conversion errors.
scenarios.role: Provides repo-specific guidance for using MarkItDown's Python API, CLI, optional converters, plugins, OCR add-on, Azure integrations, and MCP server.
scenarios.read_when: User mentions MarkItDown, markitdown CLI, MarkItDown Python API, DocumentConverter, StreamInfo, UnsupportedFormatException, MissingDependencyException, markitdown-ocr, markitdown-mcp, Azure Content Understanding, Azure Document Intelligence, or markitdown.plugin.
scenarios.best_for: Converting heterogeneous documents to Markdown for LLM pipelines, diagnosing MarkItDown conversion failures, enabling plugins/OCR, using Azure extraction, developing custom converters, and exposing conversion via local MCP.
scenarios.avoid_when: The task needs high-fidelity visual document rendering, generic OCR unrelated to MarkItDown, cloud service provisioning outside conversion, or MCP server design not using markitdown-mcp.
scenarios.selection_guidance: Choose `markitdown` over generic document/OCR/MCP skills when the user specifically uses MarkItDown package names, APIs, CLI flags, plugin entry points, converter errors, or the markitdown-mcp server. Start at the root router, then follow the workflow-specific sub-skill.

scenarios.title: Local Agent Tooling And MCP
scenarios.when_to_read: Tasks that ask an MCP client or coding agent to convert local or remote resources to Markdown using markitdown-mcp.
scenarios.role: Explains the local MarkItDown MCP server, transport choices, Docker/Claude Desktop setup, plugin enablement, and security boundaries.
scenarios.read_when: User mentions markitdown-mcp, convert_to_markdown(uri), Claude Desktop MarkItDown server, MCP Inspector, Streamable HTTP, SSE, STDIO, MARKITDOWN_ENABLE_PLUGINS, or Docker-mounted document conversion.
scenarios.best_for: Safe local/loopback MCP server setup, Docker mount guidance, plugin-enabled MarkItDown MCP conversion, and MCP troubleshooting tied to MarkItDown conversion semantics.
scenarios.avoid_when: The user wants a general authenticated public MCP service, an unrelated MCP server, or non-MarkItDown document processing.
scenarios.selection_guidance: Use the MCP sub-skill when MarkItDown is being exposed as a local agent tool; route conversion-format errors back to core-conversion and plugin setup to plugin-development or ocr-plugin.
